name: TPOT (Tree-Based Pipeline Optimization Tool)
pricing_notes: Open source (LGPL-3.0); Epistasis Lab / Vanderbilt University

performance.notes: Genetic algorithm search; minutes to hours depending on generations/population

agent_readiness.notes: Genetic programming AutoML that evolves sklearn pipelines. from tpot import TPOTClassifier. clf = TPOTClassifier(generations=5, population_size=50, verbosity=2, random_state=42). clf.fit(X_train, y_train). clf.score(X_test, y_test). clf.export('pipeline.py'). Key feature: exports Python code for the best pipeline. TPOTRegressor for regression. Parallel: n_jobs=-1. TPOT2 (beta) for newer sklearn API. Slow for large datasets. Uses DEAP for genetic algorithms. Scikit-learn compatible interface.

gotchas: Very slow — genetic search over pipeline space requires many evaluations
No type annotations — IDE support is poor
TPOT2 (in development) has breaking API changes from TPOT 0.x
Memory usage spikes during parallel population evaluation
Exports pipeline as Python code but may produce complex unreadable pipelines
best_when: When you want to export the best pipeline as Python code for production
Research into genetic programming for AutoML
One-shot automated analysis where you have hours to run
avoid_when: Time-sensitive or interactive AutoML use cases
Large datasets without significant compute resources
Production environments (use AutoGluon or FLAML instead)
summary: TPOT uses genetic programming to evolve optimal scikit-learn ML pipelines. TPOTClassifier(generations=5, population_size=50).fit(X, y). Key feature: clf.export('pipeline.py') outputs the best pipeline as standalone Python code. Very slow (hours for thorough search). LGPL-3.0. TPOT2 is the successor with breaking API changes. Best for automated pipeline discovery when you want exported, reproducible code. Prefer AutoGluon/FLAML for practical AutoML.
